Compute SHAP values for any RCausalML CATE model

Source: R/shapviz_integration.R
explain_cate.Rd

Uses kernelshap (or permshap) to compute SHAP values for the model's CATE predictions, so you can then pass the result to shapviz() and use all shapviz functions: sv_importance(), sv_dependence(), sv_waterfall(), sv_force(), sv_dependence2D(), etc.

Usage

explain_cate(
  object,
  X,
  bg_X = NULL,
  n_samples = NULL,
  use_permshap = FALSE,
  pred_fun = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

object

Fitted RCausalML model (any of the above).

X

Feature matrix or data.frame to explain (same covariates as used for fitting).

bg_X

Optional background dataset for kernelshap; if NULL, a small sample of X may be used by kernelshap (see ?kernelshap::kernelshap).

n_samples

Optional number of rows to explain (for speed). If NULL, all rows of X are used.

use_permshap

If TRUE, use kernelshap::permshap() instead of kernelshap::kernelshap() (faster, approximate). Recommended when ncol(X) > 8.

pred_fun

Optional custom prediction function of the form function(object, newdata) returning a numeric vector. If NULL, a default wrapper around predict(object, newdata) is used.

...

Passed to kernelshap::kernelshap() or kernelshap::permshap() (e.g. n_permutations, verbose).

Value

An object of class kernelshap. Pass it to shapviz() to get a shapviz object, then use sv_importance(), sv_dependence(), sv_waterfall(), sv_force(), etc.

Details

Works with all RCausalML models that have a predict(object, newdata) returning CATE (or a list with predictions), including:

  • Meta-learners: SLearner, TLearner, XLearner, RLearner, DRLearner

  • DML: LinearDML, SparseLinearDML, KernelDML, NonParamDML, CausalForestDML, DynamicDMLearner, OrthoIVLearner, DMLIVLearner, NonParamDMLIVLearner

  • Trees/forests: causal_forest, uplift_rf_*, uplift_tree_*, interaction_tree, causal_inference_tree

  • Policy: policy_learner, DRPolicyTree, DRPolicyForest

  • Interpreters: SingleTreeCateInterpreter, SingleTreePolicyInterpreter

  • Neural: cevae, dragonnet, tarnet, cfrnet, ganite

  • Other: AutomatedMLModel, driv_learner

See also

kernelshap, permshap; shapviz::shapviz(), shapviz::sv_importance(), shapviz::sv_dependence().

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
library(RCausalML)
library(kernelshap)
library(shapviz)

# Fit any CATE model
set.seed(42)
d <- synthetic_data(mode = 1, n = 500, p = 5, sigma = 1)
sl <- SLearner(learner = "ranger")
sl <- fit(sl, d$X, d$w, d$y)

# Compute SHAP for CATE predictions
X_explain <- d$X[1:100, ]   # optional: subset for speed
ks <- explain_cate(sl, X_explain, bg_X = d$X, use_permshap = TRUE)

# Build shapviz object and use all shapviz functions
shp <- shapviz(ks)
sv_importance(shp)
sv_dependence(shp, "X1")
sv_waterfall(shp, 1)
sv_force(shp, 1)
} # }
